member.name: T:LordKuper.WorkManager.Cache.PawnCache
member.summary: Caches work and skill information for a specific pawn, including work priorities, managed work types, and capability status. Provides efficient access to work-related data and supports periodic cache updates.
member.remarks: Invariant: is non-null whenever this cache is used. is created and updated only from , which is a and therefore runs only while a — and thus an active — exists. The constructor sets Instance when that game is created, before any map tick can fire. No null guard is required or expected on these paths.

member.remarks: Invariant: is non-null whenever this cache is used. is created and updated only from , which is a and therefore runs only while a — and thus an active — exists. The constructor sets Instance when that game is created, before any map tick can fire. No null guard is required or expected on these paths.

member.summary: Compares two objects and returns a value indicating whether one is less than, equal to, or greater than the other.
member.param: The first to compare.
The second to compare.
member.returns:
A signed integer that indicates the relative values of and :
Less than zero: is less than .
Zero: equals .
Greater than zero: is greater than .
Comparison is performed by:
Relevant skill count (descending).
Natural priority (descending).
Definition name (ordinal string comparison).

member.name: T:LordKuper.WorkManager.ScheduleUpdater
member.summary: Map component that assigns pawn work schedules based on configured work shifts.
member.remarks: Invariant: is non-null for the entire lifetime of this component. A can only exist while a exists, which requires an active ; the constructor runs when that game is created and sets Instance before any map tick can fire. No null guard is required or expected here.
member.param: The map this component belongs to.

member.param: The map this component belongs to.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.Settings.ValidateWorkPriorities
member.summary: Validates and adjusts work priority-related settings to ensure they fall within acceptable ranges or default values.
member.remarks: This method ensures that all work priority parameters, thresholds, and factors are properly initialized and constrained within their defined limits. Default values are applied where necessary, and invalid or out-of-range values are clamped to maintain consistency. Additionally, missing passion priorities are initialized with default values.

member.name: P:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkManagerGameComponent.Instance
member.summary:
Gets the singleton instance of the class.
Contract: Instance is valid only while a Game is loaded. It is non-null on any game-scoped path (MapComponent ticks, cache updates) because a Map can only exist when a Game — and therefore this component — exists. It may be null on game-less UI paths (mod-settings screen, main menu), and retains its last value after a game is unloaded (quit to main menu) — a stale reference. Use to guard every UI-scoped entry point; do not dereference Instance unless returns true.

member.name: P:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkManagerGameComponent.IsInitialized
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the game component has been initialized and is safe to dereference. Returns true only when both an active Game is loaded (Current.Game != null) and is non-null. Returns false on game-less UI paths (main menu, mod-settings screen opened without an active save) and after a game is unloaded (quit to main menu), even if still holds a stale reference from the previous session. Guard every UI-scoped entry point with if (!IsInitialized) return; before any dereference.

is thrown. The combined rules are stored in a dictionary where each key is a and the value is the resulting .

member.name: T:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater
member.summary: Manages and updates work priorities for pawns on a specific map.
member.remarks:
This class is responsible for dynamically assigning and updating work priorities for pawns based on various factors such as skill levels, passions, learning rates, and predefined rules. It ensures that work priorities are optimized and aligned with the current game state and configuration settings. The updates are performed periodically and take into account both global and pawn-specific conditions.
Invariant: is non-null for the entire lifetime of this component. A can only exist while a exists, which requires an active ; the constructor runs when that game is created and sets Instance before any map tick can fire. No null guard is required or expected here.

It ensures that work priorities are optimized and aligned with the current game state and configuration settings. The updates are performed periodically and take into account both global and pawn-specific conditions.
Invariant: is non-null for the entire lifetime of this component. A can only exist while a exists, which requires an active ; the constructor runs when that game is created and sets Instance before any map tick can fire. No null guard is required or expected here.

member.summary: Adds the highest-scoring workers on the clock at the given hour to the dedicated set until the hour's target count is reached or no further candidates remain.
member.param: Candidate pawns mapped to their suitability scores.
The hour of the day (0-23) being covered.
The number of dedicated workers wanted for the hour.
The accumulating set of dedicated workers (mutated).
Workers already counted toward the hour from a previous pass.
member.returns: The number of workers covering the hour after this pass.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignCommonWork
member.summary: Assigns common work priorities to managed pawns based on predefined work types and conditions.
member.remarks: This method evaluates all managed pawns and assigns work priorities for specific work types that are both relevant and compatible with the pawn's capabilities. Only pawns that are managed, capable, and not recovering are considered. Work types that are disabled or deemed unsuitable for a pawn are excluded from assignment.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignDedicatedWorkers
member.summary: Assigns dedicated workers to specific work types based on predefined rules and priorities.
member.remarks: This method evaluates the available workers and assigns them to work types that allow dedicated workers, ensuring that the target number of workers is met for each applicable work type. The assignment process considers worker suitability, existing assignments, and priority settings. If no suitable workers are available, a fallback mechanism is used to assign less optimal workers.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignDedicatedWorkersForDay(LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkTypeAssignmentRule,System.Collections.Generic.List{LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkTypeAssignmentRule})
member.summary: Assigns dedicated workers for a single rule using the legacy day-level selection: picks the highest-scoring workers for the whole day, ignoring schedules.
member.param: The work type assignment rule being satisfied.
All dedicated rules, used for scoring (dedication counts).

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignLeftoverWorkTypes
member.summary: Assigns unallocated work types to capable pawns based on their suitability and the current configuration settings.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignWorkToIdlePawns
member.summary: Assigns work priorities to pawns that are currently idle, based on managed work type rules and eligibility.
member.remarks: This method identifies pawns that have been idle for a specified duration and assigns them work priorities for eligible work types. It ensures that only managed pawns and work types that meet specific conditions (e.g., allowed and not marked as bad work) are considered. If no idle pawns are found, the method exits early.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignWorkersByLearningRate
member.summary: Assigns work priorities to managed workers based on their learning rates for specific work types.
member.remarks: This method evaluates each managed worker's learning rate for eligible work types and assigns a priority if the learning rate meets or exceeds predefined thresholds. Workers with higher learning rates are assigned higher priorities. Only workers and work types that meet specific conditions are considered for assignment.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignWorkersByPassion
member.summary: Assigns work priorities to managed workers based on their passions and predefined rules.
member.remarks: This method iterates through all capable and managed workers, evaluating their suitability for specific work types based on passion levels, work type rules, and other constraints. If a worker's passion for a work type meets the criteria and a priority is defined for that passion, the worker's priority for the work type is updated accordingly.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.AssignWorkersBySkill
member.summary: Assigns workers to tasks based on their skill levels and predefined work type rules.
member.remarks: This method evaluates the skills of available workers and assigns them to tasks where they are most proficient, following the rules defined in the managed work type configuration. Workers with higher skill levels are prioritized, and only those who meet the criteria for a specific task are considered. If no workers or relevant rules are available, the method exits without making any assignments.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.GetDedicatedWorkersScores(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{LordKuper.WorkManager.Cache.PawnCache},Verse.WorkTypeDef,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkTypeAssignmentRule})
member.summary: Calculates and returns a dictionary of scores for a collection of pawns, indicating their suitability as dedicated workers for a specified work type based on various factors.
member.remarks:
The score for each pawn is calculated based on several factors, including:
The pawn's skill level for the specified work type.
The pawn's passion for the work type.
The pawn's learning rate for the work type.
The number of other work types the pawn is dedicated to.
Each factor is normalized and weighted according to predefined settings, and the final score is computed as a weighted sum of these factors. Pawns that are not managed for the specified work type are excluded from the results.
member.param: The collection of pawns to evaluate. Cannot be .
The work type for which the scores are being calculated. Cannot be .
The collection of work type assignment rules to consider during evaluation. Cannot be .
member.returns:
A dictionary where each key is a representing a pawn, and the value is a
representing the calculated score for that pawn. Higher scores indicate greater suitability as a dedicated worker for the specified work type.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.MapComponentTick
member.summary: Executes periodic updates for work priorities and related settings in the game.
member.remarks: This method is called on each tick of the game and performs updates to work priorities if priority management is enabled and certain conditions are met. It ensures that work priorities are updated at a configurable frequency and applies the changes to all player-controlled pawns. The method also enables work priorities if they are disabled in the game settings. This method skips execution when the game paused or when the current tick does not align with the update interval. Additionally, it respects the configured update frequency to avoid unnecessary recalculations.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.TryAssignDedicatedWorkersBySchedule(LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkTypeAssignmentRule,System.Collections.Generic.List{LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkTypeAssignmentRule})
member.summary: Assigns dedicated workers for a single rule taking pawn schedules into account.
member.remarks: For every hour of the day the target worker count is recomputed from the number of capable pawns actually working that hour, and the best-scoring workers on the clock that hour are selected. The union of those workers receives the (whole-day) dedicated worker priority, so each working hour is covered by the most suitable workers available at that time. When fewer than the target good workers are on the clock, allowed-but-bad workers of that hour are used as a fail-safe.
member.param: The work type assignment rule being satisfied.
All dedicated rules, used for scoring (dedication counts).
member.returns: true if the schedule-aware selection was applied (including when there are no allowed workers); false when no allowed worker has any working hour, signalling the caller to fall back to the legacy day-level selection.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.Update
member.summary: Updates the work priorities for all player-controlled pawns, ensuring that work priorities are enabled and recalculated as necessary.
member.remarks: This method ensures that the game's work priorities setting is enabled and notifies all relevant pawns of the change. It then updates internal caches and recalculates work priorities to reflect the current state.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.UpdateCache
member.summary: Updates the internal cache of managed work types and pawn data for the current map.
member.remarks:
This method synchronizes the cache with the current state of the game, ensuring that:
Only enabled work types are tracked in the managed work types list.
All free colonists currently spawned on the map are included in the pawn list.
Stale entries in the pawn cache are removed, and new entries are added for any missing pawns.
The cache is updated to reflect the latest game state, and any necessary updates are applied to individual pawn caches.

member.name: M:LordKuper.WorkManager.WorkPriorityUpdater.UpdateWorkPriorities
member.summary: Updates the work priorities for all workers based on the current settings and configurations.
member.remarks:
This method adjusts work priorities by applying a series of rules and strategies, such as assigning common work, using dedicated workers, prioritizing by skill, passion, or learning rate, and handling leftover work types. The behavior of this method is influenced by the settings in
.
